title: Hieracium tephrosoma
intro: Hieracium tephrosoma is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), commonly known as a hawkweed. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium tephrosoma include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Hieracium tephrosoma is commonly prepared as a tea or infused into oil for topical application, and is also used as a tincture or dried herb for internal use.
taxonomy: Hieracium tephrosoma is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which includes approximately 1000 species of plants. The specific classification of Hieracium tephrosoma is further defined by its binomial nomenclature, which consists of the genus name and a unique species epithet. This binomial nomenclature is a key component in the Linnaean system of classification used in botany. Hieracium tephrosoma is a member of the plant kingdom, specifically within the division Magnoliophyta.
